get.description:
Return details on an individual issue, including its basic stats, comment
and user-report counts, and a summary of the latest event.

parameters.name: organization_id_or_slug
parameters.description: The ID or slug of the organization the resource belongs to.

parameters.name: issue_id
parameters.description: The ID of the issue you'd like to query.

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: The name of environments to filter by.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Additional data to include in the response.

parameters.name: collapse
parameters.description: Fields to remove from the response to improve query performance.

value.title: This is an example Python exception

metadata.title: This is an example Python exception

put.description:
Update an individual issue's attributes. Only the attributes submitted
are modified.

parameters.name: organization_id_or_slug
parameters.description: The ID or slug of the organization the resource belongs to.

parameters.name: issue_id
parameters.description: The ID of the issue you'd like to query.

inbox.description: If true, marks the issue as reviewed by the requestor.

status.description:
Limit mutations to only issues with the given status.

* `resolved`
* `unresolved`
* `ignored`
* `resolvedInNextRelease`
* `muted`

inNextRelease.description: If true, marks the issue as resolved in the next release.

inRelease.description: The version of the release that the issue should be resolved in.If set to `latest`, the latest release will be used.

commit.description: The SHA of the resolving commit.

repository.description: The name of the repository (as it appears in Sentry).

inCommit.description: The commit data that the issue should use for resolution.

ignoreDuration.description: Ignore the issue until for this many minutes.

ignoreCount.description: Ignore the issue until it has occurred this many times in `ignoreWindow` minutes.

ignoreWindow.description: Ignore the issue until it has occurred `ignoreCount` times in this many minutes. (Max: 1 week)

ignoreUserCount.description: Ignore the issue until it has affected this many users in `ignoreUserWindow` minutes.

ignoreUserWindow.description: Ignore the issue until it has affected `ignoreUserCount` users in this many minutes. (Max: 1 week)

statusDetails.description: Additional details about the resolution. Status detail updates that include release data are only allowed for issues within a single project.

substatus.description:
The new substatus of the issue.

* `archived_until_escalating`
* `archived_until_condition_met`
* `archived_forever`
* `escalating`
* `ongoing`
* `regressed`
* `new`

hasSeen.description: If true, marks the issue as seen by the requestor.

isBookmarked.description: If true, bookmarks the issue for the requestor.

isSubscribed.description: If true, subscribes the requestor to the issue.

merge.description: If true, merges the issues together.

discard.description: If true, discards the issues instead of updating them.

assignedTo.description: The user or team that should be assigned to the issues. Values take the form of `<user_id>`, `user:<user_id>`, `<username>`, `<user_primary_email>`, or `team:<team_id>`.

priority.description:
The priority that should be set for the issues

* `low`
* `medium`
* `high`

delete.description: Asynchronously queue an individual issue for deletion.

parameters.name: organization_id_or_slug
parameters.description: The ID or slug of the organization the resource belongs to.

parameters.name: issue_id
parameters.description: The ID of the issue you'd like to query.

get.description: Resolve a short ID to the project slug and group details.

parameters.name: organization_id_or_slug
parameters.description: The ID or slug of the organization the resource belongs to.

parameters.name: issue_id
parameters.description: The short ID of the issue to resolve.

parameters.name: collapse
parameters.description: Fields to remove from the response to improve query performance.
